title: The Brave Little Tailor
content:
On a warm summer morning, a little tailor sat at his bench sewing. A woman outside was selling jam, and the tailor bought a small jar. He set it on the windowsill. Almost at once, a swarm of flies landed in the sweet jam.

The tailor swatted with a cloth. When he lifted it, he counted seven dead flies. Seven at one blow! He was so proud he embroidered the words "Seven at One Blow" on a belt and buckled it around his waist.

He set off to seek adventure, slipping a soft cheese and a small bird into his pocket. Everyone who read his belt thought "seven" meant seven men, not seven flies!

Word reached the king about a warrior who had slain seven at one blow. The king summoned him. "I have a task for you. Two giants are terrorizing my kingdom. Defeat them and you may marry the princess."

The tailor found the two giants sleeping under a tree. He climbed up into the branches and dropped stones on one giant. The giant punched the other, thinking he had been hit. They argued, then fought each other to the ground. The tailor had done nothing but drop pebbles!

The king sent the tailor on another task: catch the unicorn that was destroying the forest. The tailor stood before a tree. When the unicorn charged, he stepped aside. The unicorn's horn drove deep into the trunk and stuck fast.

Next, the tailor was sent to catch a wild boar. He ran with the boar chasing him and spotted a small chapel. He dove through the window. The boar charged in after him. The tailor jumped back out and slammed the door shut. The boar was trapped!

The king still did not want to give up his daughter. He whispered to the princess to listen at the tailor's door at night. She heard him mutter in his sleep: "Finish that hem, boy, or I'll box your ears!"

She told her father: "He is only a tailor in his dreams!" But the whole kingdom admired him. The people cheered his name. A king cannot break his word. The little tailor married the princess, and his quick wit served the kingdom well for all his days.

author: Brothers Grimm
